title: Web archives: captured pages, crawls and WARCs
audience: Web archiving programmes, born-digital & legal-deposit teams
intro: A web archive ties content to the act that captured it. This scenario branches on a single capture vs a whole archived site, attaches the harvesting body, models the crawl as an Activity the records result from, and stores the WARC as an Instantiation.
question: Is an archived page a Record and a site a Record Set? How do I record the crawl and the WARC file?

steps.prompt: One captured page, or a whole archived site?

choices.label: A single captured page
choices.why: Good — one capture of a page is one Record (a later re-capture is another Record, or another Instantiation if you treat it as the same content).

choices.label: A whole archived site
choices.why: Good — an archived site is an aggregation of captured pages: a Record Set.

choices.label: An Activity
choices.why: Not the content. The crawl that captured it is an Activity — modelled in a later step.

choices.label: Record
choices.why: Fits. The captured page is a Record; the WARC holding it is an Instantiation; the crawl that produced it is an Activity it results from.

choices.label: Instantiation
choices.why: That's the WARC file, not the captured content. Keep the capture as a Record.

choices.label: Record Set
choices.why: Too much for one page — a Set is for the whole site.

capture.comment: Create the captured page as a Record.

choices.label: Record Set
choices.why: Fits. The archived site aggregates many captured pages — a Record Set — each page a member Record.

choices.label: Record
choices.why: Undersells it — a site is many pages captured over time. Use a Record Set.

choices.label: Instantiation
choices.why: Doesn't fit — the WARC files are Instantiations of individual captures, not the site as a whole.

capture.comment: Create the archived site as a Record Set.

steps.prompt: Attach the harvesting body (the institution that captured it).

choices.label: Agent (Corporate Body) + has_creator
choices.why: Fits. The web-archiving programme is a Corporate Body (an Agent). Relate the capture to it with has_creator; the same Agent is the creator of everything it harvests.

choices.label: The crawler's user-agent string
choices.why: That's technical metadata for the Instantiation, not the responsible body. Model the institution as an Agent.

capture.comment: Create the harvesting body as an Agent.
Link the capture to the harvester with has_creator.

steps.prompt: Capture the crawl itself — the act that produced these records.

choices.label: Activity, linked with results_from
choices.why: Fits. The crawl is an Activity with its own scope, seeds and date. Relate the records to it with results_from — provenance becomes the queryable chain record → crawl → harvester, not just a timestamp.

choices.label: A capture-date field
choices.why: Too flat. A crawl has scope, seed list, frequency and an operator; as an Activity it can link every record it produced.

capture.comment: Create the crawl as an Activity.
Link the records to the crawl with results_from.

steps.prompt: Finally, the WARC file holding the bytes.

choices.label: Instantiation
choices.why: Fits. The WARC (with its size and fixity hash) is an Instantiation of the captured Record, linked with has_instantiation. A migrated copy is simply another Instantiation.

choices.label: A separate Record
choices.why: Doesn't fit — the WARC isn't different content, it's the carrier of this capture. Model it as an Instantiation.

choices.label: An attribute on the capture
choices.why: Too flat — files have format, size and fixity and there can be several. Use Instantiations.

capture.comment: Create the WARC as an Instantiation.
Link the capture to the WARC with has_instantiation.

outcome.verdict: Capture, crawl, harvester and bytes — one chain.
outcome.summary: The page is a Record (or the site a Record Set); the harvesting body is a reusable Agent; the crawl is an Activity the records result from; and the WARC is an Instantiation. Web captures stop being anonymous files and become provenance-rich records you can search by site, crawl and date.

links.label: See real RiC data in the graph viewer
